subject: English
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the events in the story of Rakesh and the cherry tree
Analyze how patience and care help living things grow
Identify conflicts faced by the cherry tree and how it survives
Interpret the relationship between humans and nature
Develop reading comprehension, vocabulary, and critical thinking skills
Reflect on the deeper message of nurturing nature

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Nature and Plant Growth
student_activities: Discuss personal experiences of planting or caring for plants
List factors required for plant growth

lesson_plan.title: Setting and Characters
topics_covered: Introduction to Rakesh and his grandfather
The importance of the cherry seed
teaching_strategy: Guided reading of the first section
student_activities: Identify the setting of the story
Describe the relationship between Rakesh and his grandfather

lesson_plan.title: Growth of the Cherry Tree
student_activities: Arrange events of the story in sequence
Explain how the tree began to grow

lesson_plan.title: Conflicts and Challenges
topics_covered: The resilience of the cherry tree
student_activities: Discuss how the tree survived the damage
Compare the tree’s struggle with challenges in real life

lesson_plan.title: Climax and Theme
teaching_strategy: Class discussion on the story’s message
student_activities: Explain why the cherry tree is special to Rakesh
Write a paragraph about the theme of the story

lesson_plan.title: Language Skills and Revision
topics_covered: Past tense verbs used in the narrative
student_activities: Identify descriptive phrases used in the story

teaching_aids: Images of cherry trees and plant growth stages
teacher_tip: Encourage students to reflect on how small actions like planting a seed can grow into something meaningful over time. This helps students understand the deeper message of patience, care, and connection with nature.
